View this page

GEDCOM 5.x API¶

exception gedcomtools.gedcom5.parser.GedcomFormatViolationError[source]¶

Bases: Exception

Raised when a line violates the GEDCOM 5.5 format.

class gedcomtools.gedcom5.parser.Gedcom5x[source]¶

Bases: object

Low-level GEDCOM 5.x parser.

Reads a .ged file and builds a typed element tree. Typed record collections (individuals, families, etc.) and a dictionary lookup (get_element_dictionary()) are the primary outputs consumed by Gedcom5.

property individuals: List[IndividualRecord]¶
property families: List[FamilyRecord]¶
property sources: List[SourceRecord]¶
property repositories: List[RepositoryRecord]¶
property objects: List[ObjectRecord]¶
property header: List[HeaderRecord]¶
property submitters: List[SubmitterRecord]¶
get_element_dictionary()[source]¶

Return all xref-identified records keyed by uppercased xref.

Return type:

dict

get_root_child_elements()[source]¶

Return top-level records in document order.

Return type:

List[Element]

parse_file(file_path, strict=True)[source]¶

Open and parse a GEDCOM 5.x file.

Return type:

None

parse(gedcom_stream, strict=True)[source]¶

Parse a byte stream as GEDCOM 5.x data.

Resets all collections before parsing.

Return type:

None

save_gedcom(open_file)[source]¶

Write the parsed GEDCOM tree to open_file in GEDCOM 5.x format.

Return type:

None

print_gedcom()[source]¶

Write the parsed GEDCOM tree to stdout.

Return type:

None

class gedcomtools.gedcom5.elements.Element(level, xref, tag, value, crlf='\n', multi_line=True)[source]¶

Bases: object

GEDCOM element

Each line in a GEDCOM file is an element with the format

level [pointer] tag [value]

where level and tag are required, and pointer and value are optional. Elements are arranged hierarchically according to their level, and elements with a level of zero are at the top level. Elements with a level greater than zero are children of their parent.

A pointer has the format @pname@, where pname is any sequence of characters and numbers. The pointer identifies the object being pointed to, so that any pointer included as the value of any element points back to the original object. For example, an element may have a FAMS tag whose value is @F1@, meaning that this element points to the family record in which the associated person is a spouse. Likewise, an element with a tag of FAMC has a value that points to a family record in which the associated person is a child.

See a GEDCOM file for examples of tags and their values.

Tags available to an element are seen here: gedcom.tags

property parent¶
property level: int¶

Returns the level of this element from within the GEDCOM file :rtype: int

property xref¶

Returns the pointer of this element from within the GEDCOM file :rtype: str

property tag¶

Returns the tag of this element from within the GEDCOM file :rtype: str

property value¶
describe()[source]¶
Return type:

str

get_value()[source]¶

Return the value of this element from within the GEDCOM file :rtype: str

set_value(value)[source]¶

Sets the value of this element :type value: str

property crlf: str¶

Returns the line ending used for this element.

get_multi_line_value()[source]¶

Returns the value of this element including concatenations or continuations :rtype: str

set_multi_line_value(value)[source]¶

Sets the value of this element, adding concatenation and continuation lines when necessary :type value: str

sub_record(tag)[source]¶
sub_records(tag=None)[source]¶
get_child_elements()[source]¶

Returns the direct child elements of this element :rtype: list of Element

new_child_element(tag, pointer='', value='')[source]¶

Creates and returns a new child element of this element

Return type:

Element

add_child_element(element)[source]¶

Adds a child element to this element

get_parent_element()[source]¶

Returns the parent element of this element :rtype: Element

set_parent_element(element)[source]¶

Adds a parent element to this element

There’s usually no need to call this method manually, add_child_element() calls it automatically.

get_individual()[source]¶

Returns this element and all of its sub-elements represented as a GEDCOM string ::deprecated:: As of version 1.0.0 use to_gedcom_string() method instead :rtype: str

to_gedcom_string(recursive=False)[source]¶

Formats this element and optionally all of its sub-elements into a GEDCOM string :type recursive: bool :rtype: str

class gedcomtools.gedcom5.elements.RootElement(level=-1, pointer='', tag='ROOT', value='', crlf='\n', multi_line=True)[source]¶

Bases: Element

Virtual GEDCOM root element containing all logical records as children

class gedcomtools.gedcom5.elements.HeaderRecord(level, xref, tag, value, crlf='\n', multi_line=True)[source]¶

Bases: Element

class gedcomtools.gedcom5.elements.SubmitterRecord(level, xref, tag, value, crlf='\n', multi_line=True)[source]¶

Bases: Element

class gedcomtools.gedcom5.elements.FamilyRecord(level, xref, tag, value, crlf='\n', multi_line=True)[source]¶

Bases: Element

get_tag()[source]¶
class gedcomtools.gedcom5.elements.FileElement(level, xref, tag, value, crlf='\n', multi_line=True)[source]¶

Bases: Element

get_tag()[source]¶
class gedcomtools.gedcom5.elements.IndividualRecord(level, xref, tag, value, crlf='\n', multi_line=True)[source]¶

Bases: Element

get_tag()[source]¶
is_deceased()[source]¶

Checks if this individual is deceased :rtype: bool

is_child()[source]¶

Checks if this element is a child of a family :rtype: bool

is_private()[source]¶

Checks if this individual is marked private :rtype: bool

get_name()[source]¶

Returns an individual’s names as a tuple: (str given_name, str surname) :rtype: tuple

get_all_names()[source]¶
surname_match(surname_to_match)[source]¶

Matches a string with the surname of an individual :type surname_to_match: str :rtype: bool

given_match(name)[source]¶

Matches a string with the given name of an individual ::deprecated:: As of version 1.0.0 use given_name_match() method instead :type name: str :rtype: bool

given_name_match(given_name_to_match)[source]¶

Matches a string with the given name of an individual :type given_name_to_match: str :rtype: bool

get_gender()[source]¶

Returns the gender of a person in string format :rtype: str

get_birth_data()[source]¶

Returns the birth data of a person formatted as a tuple: (str date, str place, list sources) :rtype: tuple

get_birth_year()[source]¶

Returns the birth year of a person as an integer, or None if unknown. :rtype: int or None

get_death_data()[source]¶

Returns the death data of a person formatted as a tuple: (str date, str place, list sources) :rtype: tuple

get_death_year()[source]¶

Returns the death year of a person as an integer, or None if unknown. :rtype: int or None

get_burial()[source]¶

Returns the burial data of a person formatted as a tuple: (str date, str place, list sources) ::deprecated:: As of version 1.0.0 use get_burial_data() method instead :rtype: tuple

get_burial_data()[source]¶

Returns the burial data of a person formatted as a tuple: (str date, str place, list sources) :rtype: tuple

get_census()[source]¶

Returns a list of censuses of an individual formatted as tuples: (str date, str place, list sources) ::deprecated:: As of version 1.0.0 use get_census_data() method instead :rtype: list of tuple

get_census_data()[source]¶

Returns a list of censuses of an individual formatted as tuples: (str date, str place, list sources) :rtype: list of tuple

get_last_change_date()[source]¶

Returns the date of when the person data was last changed formatted as a string :rtype: str

get_occupation()[source]¶

Returns the occupation of a person :rtype: str

birth_year_match(year)[source]¶

Returns True if the given year matches the birth year of this person. Returns False if the birth year is unknown. :type year: int :rtype: bool

birth_range_match(from_year, to_year)[source]¶

Checks if the birth year of a person lies within the given range. Returns False if the birth year is unknown. :type from_year: int :type to_year: int :rtype: bool

death_year_match(year)[source]¶

Returns True if the given year matches the death year of this person. Returns False if the death year is unknown. :type year: int :rtype: bool

death_range_match(from_year, to_year)[source]¶

Checks if the death year of a person lies within the given range. Returns False if the death year is unknown. :type from_year: int :type to_year: int :rtype: bool

criteria_match(criteria)[source]¶

Checks if this individual matches all of the given criteria

criteria is a colon-separated list, where each item in the list has the form [name]=[value]. The following criteria are supported:

surname=[name]

Match a person with [name] in any part of the surname.

given_name=[given_name]

Match a person with [given_name] in any part of the given given_name.

birth=[year]

Match a person whose birth year is a four-digit [year].

birth_range=[from_year-to_year]

Match a person whose birth year is in the range of years from [from_year] to [to_year], including both [from_year] and [to_year].

Return type:

bool

describe()[source]¶

Return a human-readable description of this individual. Includes name, gender, birth/death data, and occupation if available.

Return type:

str

class gedcomtools.gedcom5.elements.ObjectRecord(level, xref, tag, value, crlf='\n', multi_line=True)[source]¶

Bases: Element

is_object()[source]¶

Checks if this element is an actual object :rtype: bool

class gedcomtools.gedcom5.elements.RepositoryRecord(level, xref, tag, value, crlf='\n', multi_line=True)[source]¶

Bases: Element

class gedcomtools.gedcom5.elements.SourceRecord(level, xref, tag, value, crlf='\n', multi_line=True)[source]¶

Bases: Element

gedcomtools.gedcom5.helpers.deprecated(func)[source]¶

This is a decorator which can be used to mark functions as deprecated. It will result in a warning being emitted when the function is used.

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_PROGRAM_DEFINED_TAG_MREL = '_MREL'¶

_MREL

Relationship to a mother.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_PROGRAM_DEFINED_TAG_FREL = '_FREL'¶

_FREL

Relationship to a father.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_BIRTH = 'BIRT'¶

BIRT

The event of entering into life.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_BURIAL = 'BURI'¶

BURI

The event of the proper disposing of the mortal remains of a deceased person.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_CENSUS = 'CENS'¶

CENS.

The event of the periodic count of the population for a designated locality, such as a national or state Census.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_CHANGE = 'CHAN'¶

CHAN

Indicates a change, correction, or modification. Typically used in connection with a gedcom.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_DATE to specify when a change in information occurred.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_CHILD = 'CHIL'¶

CHIL

The natural, adopted, or sealed (LDS) child of a father and a mother.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_CONCATENATION = 'CONC'¶

CONC

An indicator that additional data belongs to the superior value. The information from the CONC value is to be connected to the value of the superior preceding line without a space and without a carriage return and/or new line character. Values that are split for a CONC tag must always be split at a non-space. If the value is split on a space the space will be lost when concatenation takes place. This is because of the treatment that spaces get as a GEDCOM delimiter, many GEDCOM values are trimmed of trailing spaces and some systems look for the first non-space starting after the tag to determine the beginning of the value.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_CONTINUED = 'CONT'¶

CONT

An indicator that additional data belongs to the superior value. The information from the CONT value is to be connected to the value of the superior preceding line with a carriage return and/or new line character. Leading spaces could be important to the formatting of the resultant text. When importing values from CONT lines the reader should assume only one delimiter character following the CONT tag. Assume that the rest of the leading spaces are to be a part of the value.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_DATE = 'DATE'¶

DATE

The time of an event in a calendar format.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_DEATH = 'DEAT'¶

DEAT

The event when mortal life terminates.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_FAMILY = 'FAM'¶

FAM.

Identifies a legal, common law, or other customary relationship of man and woman and their children, if any, or a family created by virtue of the birth of a child to its biological father and mother.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_FAMILY_CHILD = 'FAMC'¶

FAMC

Identifies the family in which an individual appears as a child.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_FAMILY_SPOUSE = 'FAMS'¶

FAMS

Identifies the family in which an individual appears as a spouse.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_FILE = 'FILE'¶

FILE

An information storage place that is ordered and arranged for preservation and reference.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_GIVEN_NAME = 'GIVN'¶

GIVN

A given or earned name used for official identification of a person.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_HEADER = 'HEAD'¶

HEAD

gedcom Header information.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_HUSBAND = 'HUSB'¶

HUSB

An individual in the family role of a married man or father.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_INDIVIDUAL = 'INDI'¶

INDI

A person.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_MARRIAGE = 'MARR'¶

MARR.

A legal, common-law, or customary event of creating a family unit of a man and a woman as husband and wife.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_NAME = 'NAME'¶

NAME.

A word or combination of words used to help identify an individual, title, or other item. More than one NAME line should be used for people who were known by multiple names.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_OBJECT = 'OBJE'¶

OBJE

Pertaining to a grouping of attributes used in describing something. Usually referring to the data required to represent a multimedia object, such an audio recording, a photograph of a person, or an image of a document.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_OCCUPATION = 'OCCU'¶

OCCU

The type of work or profession of an individual.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_PEDI = 'PEDI'¶

PEDI

Information pertaining to the pedigree (lineage) of an individual. Common values: birth, adopted, foster, sealing.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_PLACE = 'PLAC'¶

PLAC

A jurisdictional name to identify the place or location of an event.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_PRIVATE = 'PRIV'¶

PRIV

Flag for private address or event.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_SEX = 'SEX'¶

SEX

Indicates the sex of an individual–male or female.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_SOURCE = 'SOUR'¶

SOUR

The initial or original material from which information was obtained.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_SURNAME = 'SURN'¶

SURN

A family name passed on or used by members of a family.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_SUBMITTER = 'SUBM'¶

SUBM

A submitter record identifying the person or organization that contributed the genealogical data.

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_REPOSITORY = 'REPO'¶

REPO

A repository record identifying where a source is stored (archive, library, etc.).

Type:

Value

gedcomtools.gedcom5.tags.GEDCOM_TAG_WIFE = 'WIFE'¶

WIFE

An individual in the role as a mother and/or married woman.

Type:

Value
